rulings.Case Title: CHARLES GILPIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendant The Goodyear Tire Rubber Company'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co.¿s person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and request for sanctions is continued to April 18, 2012 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 503 per agreement of the parties. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Motion And Motion To Compel Textron, Inc.S Deposition And Production Of Documents Or Tangible Things And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel Textron, Inc.¿s deposition and production of documents or tangible things is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Gow Supply Company¿S Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs¿ Production Of Memorial Video And For The Imposition Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendants Abco Mechanical Contractors, Inc. and York International Corporation¿s joinder in Gow Supply Company¿s motion for order compelling plaintiffs¿ production of memorial video and for sanctions is denied as untimely. Gow Supply took its underlying motion off calendar and defendants¿ joinder failed to provide proper notice required by C.C.P. 1005(b) and C.C.P. 1010.6. The hearing date was set for April 11 and defendants electronically served their joinder on March 19. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Deft Gow Supply Company'S Mo For Order Compelling Pltfs' Production Of Memorial Video And For The Imposition Of Sanctions; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, April 17, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendants The Sherwin Williams Company and Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s joinder in Gow Supply Company¿s motion for order compelling plaintiffs¿ production of memorial video is granted. No opposition filed. Although Gow Supply took its motion off calendar, defendants¿ joinder was timely and provided proper notice and plaintiffs did not oppose. Plaintiffs have 14 days from the date of this hearing to produce the video. The request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
